error.signature: AI tells someone in Norway to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Norway does not use 911, and 112 is specifically the police number here. Dial 110 for fire, 112 for police, and 113 for ambulance/medical. All are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Norway expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Norway. Fire is 110, police is 112, and ambulance is 113. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume 112 is for medical emergencies as in the EU single-number model
dead_ends.why_fails: In Norway 112 is the police number; medical emergencies go to 113 and fire to 110. Calling 112 for a medical case delays the ambulance.

workarounds.action: For a medical emergency dial 113 directly; 110 for fire, 112 for police.
workarounds.how: 113 ambulance, 110 fire, 112 police; operators generally speak English.
